verses.1: Job again took up his parable, and said,
verses.2: “As God lives, who has taken away my right,
verses.3: (for the length of my life is still in me,
verses.4: surely my lips will not speak unrighteousness,
verses.5: Far be it from me that I should justify you.
verses.6: I hold fast to my righteousness, and will not let it go.
verses.7: “Let my enemy be as the wicked.
verses.8: For what is the hope of the godless, when he is cut off,
verses.9: Will God hear his cry when trouble comes on him?
verses.10: Will he delight himself in the Almighty,
verses.11: I will teach you about the hand of God.
verses.12: Behold, all of you have seen it yourselves;
verses.13: “This is the portion of a wicked man with God,
verses.14: If his children are multiplied, it is for the sword.
verses.15: Those who remain of him will be buried in death.
verses.16: Though he heap up silver as the dust,
verses.17: he may prepare it, but the just will put it on,
verses.18: He builds his house as the moth,
verses.19: He lies down rich, but he will not do so again.
verses.21: The east wind carries him away, and he departs.
verses.22: For it hurls at him, and does not spare,
verses.23: Men will clap their hands at him,
